# Sub-Plan 01: Engine Scaffold, Body Model & Render Loop

> Part of the **Chompy Asteroids** master plan (web 2D asteroid game). This sub-plan builds the
> greenfield project scaffold and the rendering spine ONLY. Physics, collisions, gravity, input,
> and world population are later sub-plans and are explicit non-goals here.

## Context

The repo is greenfield: the working directory holds only `.plan-tree/` metadata and a
single-file Canvas prototype at `.plan-tree/prototype/index.html` (~570 lines, all globals in one
IIFE). That prototype is the **executable spec**. There is no `package.json`, source tree, build
tooling, engine, test runner, or git.

This sub-plan stands up the project and ports the prototype's **render spine** — the parts that
draw the world — onto a PixiJS render-only foundation, while establishing the
`Workflows / Operations / Connectors` module seam the prototype lacks and **freezing the canonical
`Body` contract** that every later sub-plan depends on. Rendering is driven by mock/stub bodies;
no simulation runs yet.

## Engine decision (confirmed): PixiJS, render-only

The prototype's physics is hand-rolled and engine-agnostic. We keep that custom sim (built in
later sub-plans) and use **PixiJS purely as the GPU render adapter**. Rationale recorded in the
repo README:
- Phaser would bundle Arcade/Matter physics that directly fight the bespoke velocity-regime
  collision model — unused weight and conceptual conflict.
- PixiJS gives batched GPU rendering with no physics opinion; asteroids map cleanly to a
  `PIXI.Graphics` cleared+redrawn per frame (silhouettes mutate at runtime via dents, and the
  soft-body squash is a per-body affine — both native to Pixi's transform/Graphics API).

## Tech stack

- **Vite** (vanilla-js template) as bundler + dev server (the visual verification harness).
- **PixiJS** (`pixi.js`, v8) as the render adapter.
- **Vitest** installed and configured now (scaffold only) so Sub-Plan 02 has a test runner ready;
  this sub-plan adds only trivial smoke tests (see Verification), not physics tests.
- **git** initialized with a Node `.gitignore` (ignore `node_modules`, `dist`).

## Module layout

```
package.json, vite.config.js, vitest.config.js, .gitignore, index.html, README.md
src/
  main.js                      # Connector: bootstraps Pixi app, owns the RAF loop shell
  config.js                    # render-spine tuning constants (see below)
  domain/
    Body.js                    # FROZEN canonical Body contract: makeBody() factory + field doc
  Workflows/
    GameLoop.js                # dt-plumbed try/catch frame loop; calls step()(stub) then render()
  Operations/
    .gitkeep                   # (physics lives here in Sub-Plan 02 — empty placeholder now)
  Connectors/
    PixiRenderer.js            # world container, per-body Graphics, drawAst port, starfield, camera apply
    Starfield.js               # screen-space parallax tiling (3 layers, modulo wrap)
    Hud.js                     # DOM HUD shell: reads a view-model, writes text/among elements
  mock/
    mockWorld.js               # a handful of static Body stubs + a designated "player" body for camera
```

Boundary intent (volatility split): `Connectors/` know Pixi/DOM; `domain/Body.js` is plain data;
`Workflows/GameLoop.js` orchestrates and is render/sim-agnostic via injected `step`/`render`
callbacks. This sub-plan injects a **no-op `step`** so the seam exists for Sub-Plan 02 to fill.

## The frozen `Body` contract (`src/domain/Body.js`)

Port `makeAst` (prototype lines 120–129) verbatim in shape. `makeBody({...})` returns:

| field | kind | meaning / source |
|-------|------|------------------|
| `id` | sim | unique id (monotonic counter) |
| `player` | sim flag, read by render | true for the player body (render tints + center dot) |
| `dead` | sim | death/removal flag (unused by render this plan) |
| `x`, `y` | render + sim | world-space position |
| `vx`, `vy` | sim | velocity (unused by render this plan; present in contract) |
| `m` | sim + render input | mass; render derives radius from it |
| `r` | **derived, NOT stored** | always `MASS_TO_R(m)` — a function, never a field |
| `rot`, `vr` | render (sim updates vr) | rotation angle and angular velocity |
| `verts[]` | render + sim | 9 radial multipliers (~0.78–1.18); silhouette, mutated by dents later |
| `hue` | render | base color hue (player=195, drifters 20–50) |
| `def`, `dvel`, `dax`, `day` | render + sim | soft-body squash magnitude, spring velocity, world axis (x,y) |
| `flash` | render + sim | contact/crack rim-flash intensity, fades each frame |

Notes baked into the file as comments:
- `r` is **derived** via `MASS_TO_R` (config) — do not add a stored `r`; later plans must not
  introduce one.
- `player` is a sim-owned flag the renderer reads — it straddles layers; documented as such.
- `makeBody` uses the finite-guard (`fin`) on numeric inputs (port the prototype's NaN guard) and
  defaults a sensible mass so a malformed stub can't poison rendering.

## Render-spine constants (`src/config.js`)

Port ONLY the render-relevant constants; everything else is a later sub-plan's:
- `MASS_TO_R = m => 14 + Math.pow(Math.max(0,m),0.37)*2.5` (prototype line 52)
- `DEF_MAX=0.55`, `DEF_SPRING=0.22`, `DEF_DAMP=0.78` (lines 100–102) — included for contract
  completeness even though the spring is integrated in Sub-Plan 02; render only reads `def/dax/day`.
- Starfield layer table (lines 498–502) and camera follow/zoom constants (`zoom floor 0.7`,
  `120/r`, lerp `0.06`, lines 444–448) live in config so Sub-Plan 04 can tune them.
- Camera zoom in this sub-plan is **mock-driven** (follow a designated body, apply the same
  zoom-from-radius formula) so the camera-follow + zoom path is exercised without real growth.

Physics/world constants (G, GRAV_*, V_MERGE, ABSORB_*, POP, MAX_BODIES, spawn margins, …) are
**NOT** ported here — they belong to Sub-Plans 02/03.

## Rendering port details (Canvas-2D → PixiJS)

`Connectors/PixiRenderer.js` reproduces the prototype's `render()` + `drawAst()` (lines 461–540):
- A **world container** whose `scale = cam.z` and `position = (-cam.x*cam.z, -cam.y*cam.z)`
  reproduces `scale(cam.z); translate(-cam.x,-cam.y)` (line 527). Resolve the exact Pixi transform
  so a world point maps identically to the prototype (verify with one hand-checked coordinate).
- Each body owns a persistent `PIXI.Graphics`; per frame: `clear()`, rebuild the 9-vertex polygon
  (lines 478–481), fill via `hsl(...)` → convert to Pixi color/alpha, stroke (lines 482–485).
  The big-body purple gravity-halo gradient (lines 475–477) is **deferred to a later plan** (it
  keys on `m>500`, a physics concern) — note this explicitly; render spine draws the body only.
- **Soft-body squash** is applied as the body container's transform, NOT inside the polygon
  rebuild: set container rotation/scale to reproduce `rotate(ang); scale(1-def,1+def*0.5);
  rotate(-ang); rotate(rot)` (lines 468–474). Since Pixi can't nest two rotations on one node the
  way the canvas matrix stacks them, use a **two-node nest**: an outer "deform" node carrying the
  axis-aligned squash and an inner "body" node carrying `rot`. Document this as the one structural
  divergence from the canvas matrix and verify it visually against a known-squashed stub.
- `flash` rim glow (lines 487–491) → an additive (`BLEND_MODES.ADD`) restroke at `alpha=flash`.
- Player center dot (line 493) and player tint (line 482–485) preserved.
- Draw order: bodies sorted ascending by mass (line 530) so big bodies render on top.
- Particles and shock rings (lines 528, 532–538) are collision FX → **stubbed empty arrays** this
  plan; the renderer iterates them (zero items) so the integration point exists.

`Connectors/Starfield.js` ports `starLayers`/`starPos`/`drawStars` (lines 498–522) into a
**screen-space** container that is NOT under the world transform: 3 layers, per-layer modulo
tiling driven by `cam.x*cam.z*depth` (lines 511–518). Use Pixi `Graphics`/`ParticleContainer` of
small rects. This must tile seamlessly as the camera moves arbitrarily far in any direction
(positive-modulo math ported exactly).

`Connectors/Hud.js` ports the DOM HUD (markup lines 26–35, updates 542–559) but reads from a plain
**view-model object** the loop hands it (`{total, biggest, eaten, merged, shatter, mass, rank,
growthPct}`), not from globals. This plan feeds it mock/placeholder values (counts of mock bodies,
zeros for eaten/merged/shatter). The game-over overlay branch (lines 555–559) is ported as dormant
markup (a later plan wires the death path).

## The loop shell (`src/Workflows/GameLoop.js` + `src/main.js`)

- Port the try/catch RAF loop (lines 562–567) **verbatim in spirit**: each frame is wrapped so one
  thrown error is logged and the loop survives (`requestAnimationFrame` re-armed unconditionally).
- **Plumb `dt` now** even though no physics consumes it: compute `dt` from successive
  `performance.now()` timestamps, clamp it to a sane max (e.g. ≤ ~2 frames to avoid spiral-of-death
  after a tab stall), and pass it into the injected `step(dt)` (no-op stub here) and a
  `tick(dt)` for the renderer's frame-time-independent bits. Master plan mandates a dt-based sim;
  establishing the dt source in the loop shell is in-scope so Sub-Plans 02/03 inherit it.
- `main.js` creates the Pixi `Application`, mounts its canvas, builds the renderer/starfield/HUD,
  loads `mock/mockWorld.js`, and starts the loop with `step = () => {}` (no-op) and
  `render = (dt) => renderer.draw(mockBodies, cam, hudVM)`.

## Mock world (`src/mock/mockWorld.js`)

Exports ~6–10 `makeBody`-shaped stubs spread across world space with varied `m`, `hue`, `verts`,
and a designated `player:true` body the camera follows. Include at least one body with a nonzero
`def`/`dax`/`day` to exercise the squash render path, and one with `flash>0` to exercise the rim
glow. Bodies may carry a tiny static `rot`/drift applied in `render`'s `tick(dt)` purely so the
camera-follow and parallax are visibly exercised — this is presentation only, not simulation
(documented as such; no gravity/collision).

## Verification (visual + smoke; no physics tests this plan)

1. **Dev server runs:** `npm run dev` serves the app; the canvas shows the dark field, tiled
   starfield, and the mock asteroids with correct silhouettes/colors. No console errors.
2. **Camera follows + zooms:** the designated player stub is centered; driving its mock position
   (temporary keyboard nudge or scripted drift) shows the camera lerp-follow and the
   radius-based zoom path both fire. Removing the player stub shows the dormant game-over overlay
   markup appears (death path placeholder), confirming the branch is wired but inert.
3. **Starfield tiles infinitely:** programmatically jump `cam.x/cam.y` to large +/- magnitudes
   (e.g. ±1e6) and confirm the parallax layers still fill the screen with no gaps/seams and no
   NaN — the positive-modulo tiling is correct in all directions.
4. **Soft-body squash renders:** the stub with nonzero `def` visibly squashes along its
   `(dax,day)` axis and (since the spring isn't integrated yet) holds that shape — confirming the
   two-node deform-nest reproduces the canvas affine. Hand-verify one world→screen coordinate
   matches the prototype's transform.
5. **Sustained run:** leave it running several minutes; the try/catch loop logs nothing, frame
   rate is stable, memory is flat (Graphics are reused/cleared, not recreated per frame).
6. **Smoke tests (Vitest):** `makeBody` returns all frozen fields with finite numbers and applies
   the NaN guard (pass `NaN` mass → falls back); `MASS_TO_R` is monotonic increasing. These prove
   the test harness works for Sub-Plan 02. Each assertion shown to fail if the guard/derivation is
   broken.

Chrome DevTools MCP may be used to screenshot the running canvas for the camera/starfield/squash
checks; if unavailable, verify in a normal browser.

## Hand-off artifacts for later sub-plans

- **Frozen `Body` contract** (`src/domain/Body.js`) — Sub-Plan 02 builds physics against it.
- **`step(dt)` injection seam** in `GameLoop` — Sub-Plan 02 supplies the real per-frame sim.
- **Empty `particles`/`shocks` arrays + `Operations/` placeholder** — collision FX/logic land here.
- **Config split** — render constants in place; physics/world constants intentionally absent so
  later plans add their own without colliding.

## Non-goals (restated)

No gravity, collision regimes, soft-body spring integration, consume/growth, input handling, world
population/spawn/cull, particle simulation, or the big-body gravity halo. Mock data only.
